code_dossier.name: HTMLFilter.java
code_dossier.description: A comprehensive HTML/XSS filter utility that sanitizes user-submitted HTML content by whitelisting allowed tags, attributes, and protocols while escaping or removing potentially malicious content. This is a critical security component for preventing Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks.

data.detailed_description:
HTMLFilter is a comprehensive XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) prevention utility class that implements a whitelist-based HTML sanitization strategy. The class is designed to filter user-submitted HTML content, allowing only safe, pre-approved HTML elements and attributes while neutralizing potentially dangerous content.

**Core Architecture:**
The filter operates through a multi-stage pipeline:
1. **Comment Escaping**: HTML comments are escaped to prevent comment-based attacks
2. **HTML Balancing**: Unbalanced angle brackets are either fixed or escaped based on configuration
3. **Tag Processing**: Each HTML tag is validated against a whitelist of allowed tags and attributes
4. **Blank Removal**: Empty tags that might be used for exploits are removed
5. **Entity Validation**: HTML entities are validated to prevent entity-based attacks

**Configuration System:**
The class supports three construction modes:
- Default constructor with sensible security defaults (allows basic formatting tags: a, img, b, strong, i, em)
- Debug mode constructor for development/troubleshooting
- Map-based configuration for fully customized filtering rules

**Security Features:**
- Protocol validation on href/src attributes (only http, https, mailto allowed)
- HTML entity encoding to prevent script injection
- Quote encoding to prevent attribute injection
- Self-closing tag enforcement for elements like img
- Tag counting to ensure proper nesting and closure

**Pattern Matching:**
Uses 20+ pre-compiled regex patterns stored as static final constants for performance. Includes patterns for detecting comments, tags, attributes (quoted/unquoted), protocols, entities, and various edge cases. Thread-safe caching of dynamically generated patterns using ConcurrentHashMap.
responsibilities: Sanitize user-submitted HTML content to prevent XSS attacks by filtering out malicious or disallowed HTML elements and attributes
Validate and whitelist HTML tags, ensuring only pre-approved elements (a, img, b, strong, i, em by default) are allowed through
Process and validate URL protocols in href/src attributes, blocking dangerous protocols while allowing http, https, and mailto
Handle HTML entity encoding/decoding to prevent entity-based injection attacks and ensure proper character escaping
Balance and normalize HTML structure by managing tag opening/closing and handling malformed markup

interfaces.name: HTMLFilter
interfaces.description: Default constructor with secure defaults: allows a, img, b, strong, i, em tags with limited attributes

interfaces.name: HTMLFilter
interfaces.description: Debug mode constructor that enables verbose logging of filter operations

interfaces.name: HTMLFilter

parameters.name: conf
parameters.description: Configuration map with keys: vAllowed, vSelfClosingTags, vNeedClosingTags, vDisallowed, vAllowedProtocols, vProtocolAtts, vRemoveBlanks, vAllowedEntities

interfaces.description: Fully configurable constructor allowing custom whitelist rules

interfaces.name: filter

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: Raw HTML input string potentially containing malicious content

interfaces.description: Main entry point that filters input HTML and returns sanitized output with only whitelisted elements

interfaces.name: isAlwaysMakeTags
interfaces.description: Returns whether the filter attempts to form valid HTML from unbalanced brackets

interfaces.name: isStripComments
interfaces.description: Returns whether HTML comments are stripped from output

interfaces.name: htmlSpecialChars
interfaces.description: Static utility method that escapes HTML special characters (&, <, >, ") to their entity equivalents

interfaces.name: chr
interfaces.description: Static utility converting decimal code point to character string (PHP chr() equivalent)

interfaces.name: escapeComments
interfaces.description: Escapes HTML comment content to prevent comment-based attacks

interfaces.name: balanceHTML
interfaces.description: Balances HTML by fixing or escaping unmatched angle brackets

interfaces.name: checkTags
interfaces.description: Validates all HTML tags against whitelist and appends missing closing tags

interfaces.name: processRemoveBlanks
interfaces.description: Removes empty tags that could be used for exploits

interfaces.name: processTag
interfaces.description: Processes individual tag, validating name and attributes against whitelist

interfaces.name: processParamProtocol
interfaces.description: Validates URL protocol, converting disallowed protocols to anchor links

interfaces.name: decodeEntities
interfaces.description: Decodes HTML entities (decimal, hex, URL-encoded) to detect obfuscated attacks

interfaces.name: validateEntities
interfaces.description: Validates HTML entities against whitelist, escaping invalid ones

interfaces.name: encodeQuotes
interfaces.description: Encodes quote characters outside of tags to prevent attribute injection

interfaces.name: checkEntity
interfaces.description: Validates individual entity and returns escaped or original form

interfaces.name: isValidEntity
interfaces.description: Checks if entity is in allowed entities list

interfaces.name: inArray
interfaces.description: Static utility checking if string exists in array

interfaces.name: allowed
interfaces.description: Checks if tag name is allowed based on whitelist and blacklist

interfaces.name: allowedAttribute
interfaces.description: Checks if attribute is allowed for the specified tag

interfaces.name: regexReplace
interfaces.description: Static utility performing regex replacement

interfaces.name: reset
interfaces.description: Resets tag counts for new filter operation

interfaces.name: debug
interfaces.description: Logs debug message if debug mode is enabled
